target.title: feat(ci): guard dependency graph changes in PRs
target.body:
## Summary

- Replaces the inline dependency-awareness workflow logic with a checked-in Node script that runs from the trusted base checkout under `pull_request_target`.
- Labels and comments for dependency graph surfaces: package lockfiles, workspace/patch dependency policy files, and package manifest fields that can affect dependency resolution or install behavior.
- Hard-fails dependency graph changes unless an explicit `OPENCLAW_SECURITY_APPROVERS` allowlist member authorizes the current guard state with `/allow-dependencies-change`.
- Keeps `dependencies-changed` as the only dependency label; the hard block is the failing check plus the guard comment.
- Preserves the guard comment as a freshness barrier when graph changes are removed, so old allow comments cannot authorize later reintroduced graph changes.

## Notes

- Script-only `package.json` changes do not get the dependency label and do not block.
- `/allow-dependencies-change` is only a comment token consumed on the next workflow run or manual re-run. It does not trigger an action or add labels by itself.
- The override records the current head SHA when accepted. After authorization, a later push requires a fresh `/allow-dependencies-change` comment.
- This PR adds the failing check behavior, but repository rulesets/branch protection still need to require the `Dependency Change Awareness` check before it becomes an enforced merge gate.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.scripts.json scripts/github/dependency-change-awareness.mjs`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/dependency-change-awareness-workflow.test.ts test/scripts/dependency-change-awareness-script.test.ts`
- `git diff --check -- .github/workflows/dependency-change-awareness.yml scripts/github/dependency-change-awareness.mjs test/scripts/dependency-change-awareness-workflow.test.ts test/scripts/dependency-change-awareness-script.test.ts`

target.title: fix(auth): harden Codex auth probes
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Makes `models status --probe --probe-provider openai-codex --probe-profile <id>` run as a raw OpenClaw model probe with the selected OAuth profile instead of inheriting the normal Codex harness/session path.
- Ensures native Codex Responses probe payloads still send non-empty instructions when raw probe mode suppresses the normal system prompt.
- Keeps the change narrow: no stream-resolution changes, no config/schema changes, no generated output.

Why does this matter now?

The auth probe is meant to answer whether a selected profile can complete a minimal model round-trip. If the probe runs through the normal session harness or sends an empty native Responses instruction field, it can fail or exercise a different path than the status command is trying to validate.

What is the intended outcome?

A selected `openai-codex` OAuth profile can be probed through the built-in OpenClaw model-run path, while native Codex Responses receives a small default instruction only when the raw probe would otherwise produce no instructions.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Existing session continuation and stream-resolution ownership.
- General provider discovery or credential-store migration behavior.
- Non-native/custom Codex-compatible Responses backends.

What does success look like?

The CLI probe reports `status: "ok"` for the selected profile, focused regression tests cover the call shape and payload fallback, and existing stream-resolution tests continue to pass.

What should reviewers focus on?

The runtime/auth fields passed by the probe (`agentHarnessRuntimeOverride`, `modelRun`, `authProfileId`) and the native-only scoping of the default instructions fallback.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Does not close an issue.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #75272. This branch is a current-main narrow implementation for the auth-probe path and intentionally avoids changing stream resolution.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No maintainer request.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed:

`models status --probe` for a selected `openai-codex` OAuth profile should exercise a raw OpenClaw model-run probe and complete a minimal round-trip.

- Real environment tested:

Local synthetic-current-main checkout: this patch applied cleanly on top of `upstream/main` `bd6a404aa32357c21d6588a35b18e1034fe4b610` at proof time, using an existing local OAuth profile and a minimal config file at `/tmp/openclaw-empty-config.json` to avoid unrelated legacy config fields. Final pre-push fetch later moved `upstream/main` to `05f357b13bb348f408b9a13a84e675ffe5dbd38f`; the branch still had zero touched-file overlap with base changes and `git merge-tree --write-tree upstream/main HEAD` was clean.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-empty-config.json \
  node --import tsx src/entry.ts models status \
    --agent main \
    --probe \
    --probe-provider openai-codex \
    --probe-profile openai-codex:default \
    --probe-timeout 90000 \
    --probe-max-tokens 16 \
    --json
```

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

Terminal capture from the synthetic-current-main checkout, copied live output with secrets redacted and only the public-safe probe result included:

```text
exit 0
duration_s 88.61
stdout_bytes 6802
stderr_bytes 77
stdout_prefix_lines ['[agents/auth-profiles] using external OAuth credential after refresh [REDACTED]']
probe_summary.results[0]:
{
  "provider": "openai-codex",
  "model": "openai-codex/gpt-5.4",
  "profileId": "openai-codex:default",
  "label": "openai-codex:default",
  "source": "profile",
  "mode": "oauth",
  "status": "ok",
  "latencyMs": 12958
}
proof_ok True
```

- Observed result after fix:

The command exited 0 and the parsed probe result for `openai-codex:default` reported `status: "ok"` on a checkout with this patch applied to `upstream/main` at proof time. The later final pre-push fresh-base check found no touched-file overlap and a clean merge-tree against `05f357b13bb348f408b9a13a84e675ffe5dbd38f`.

- What was not tested:

I did not test every provider profile combination, expired/invalid OAuth recovery, or hosted CI before opening. The real run used one local `openai-codex:default` OAuth profile.

- Proof limitations or environment constraints:

The local raw stdout also contained one auth-profile refresh warning before the JSON object, so the evidence above is the sanitized terminal summary and probe-result excerpt rather than the full raw status output. The warning was unrelated to this patch and the parsed probe result completed successfully.

- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):

Before the patch, focused RED checks showed the raw Codex Responses payload could have `instructions === undefined`, and the probe runner did not pass raw model-run/runtime override fields for the `openai-codex` probe path.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

```bash
git diff --check -- src/commands/models/list.probe.ts src/commands/models/list.probe.test.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/commands/models/list.probe.ts src/commands/models/list.probe.test.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/models/list.probe.test.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/stream-resolution.test.ts --root /tmp/openclaw-codex-auth-probe-20260528135601
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts src/agents/harness/runtime-plugin.test.ts --root /tmp/openclaw-codex-auth-probe-20260528135601

# Additional drift proof on upstream/main at proof time (bd6a404aa3) with this patch applied:
git apply --check /tmp/openclaw-codex-auth-probe-current-main.patch
git diff --check --cached -- src/commands/models/list.probe.ts src/commands/models/list.probe.test.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/commands/models/list.probe.ts src/commands/models/list.probe.test.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/models/list.probe.test.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/stream-resolution.test.ts --root /tmp/openclaw-codex-auth-probe-mergeproof-20260528150011
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts src/agents/harness/runtime-plugin.test.ts --root /tmp/openclaw-codex-auth-probe-mergeproof-20260528150011
```

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `list.probe.test.ts` now asserts the selected `openai-codex` profile is forwarded with raw OpenClaw model-run fields.
- `openai-transport-stream.test.ts` covers native fallback instructions and confirms custom/proxy Codex-compatible Responses backends are not changed.

What failed before this fix, if known?

The new focused checks failed before the production changes: the probe call lacked the raw model-run/runtime override fields, and the native raw Responses payload had no instructions.

If no test was added, why not?

Tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes, for `models status --probe` when probing selected `openai-codex` profiles.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes, auth probe execution now pins the built-in runtime path and preserves the selected OAuth profile explicitly. It does not add new credential storage or logging.

What is the highest-risk area?

Accidentally changing normal Codex session stream ownership or applying the fallback instructions to custom/proxy backends.

How is that risk mitigated?

The patch does not touch `stream-resolution.ts`; existing stream-resolution tests still pass. The fallback instructions are gated by native backend detection, with a regression test proving custom/proxy backends still omit the fallback.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review and CI after the PR is opened.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

No known author-side code blocker. CI has not run on this branch yet; local drift proof applied this patch to `upstream/main` at proof time and reran the focused checks plus real CLI probe. Final pre-push fresh-base check against `05f357b13bb348f408b9a13a84e675ffe5dbd38f` had zero touched-file overlap and a clean merge-tree.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

No comments on this PR yet.

target.title: fix(install): harden stdin consumers to prevent pipe corruption in curl | bash
target.body:
## Problem

When invoked via `curl -fsSL https://openclaw.ai/install.sh | bash`, bash reads the script from stdin. Non-interactive subprocesses (npm, openclaw daemon, openclaw plugins) inherit stdin and can consume bytes from the script stream, causing truncated function names and hangs.

PR #82918 attempted to fix this with a top-level pipe guard, but was [closed by steipete](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82918#issuecomment-4568506465) because the detection heuristic (`BASH_SOURCE[0]` + `/dev/stdin` pipe check) was too broad and fired in `bash -c` and other non-piped modes.

## Fix

Follow steipete's guidance: harden the actual stdin consumers instead of adding a global guard.

- **Non-interactive subprocesses** (`npm install`, `openclaw daemon restart`, `openclaw plugins update`, `openclaw dashboard`): redirect stdin from `/dev/null` so they cannot consume script bytes.
- **Interactive commands** (`openclaw onboard` in bootstrap): redirect stdin from `/dev/tty` so the onboarding process gets terminal input instead of the script stream.
- **Wrapper functions** (`run_with_spinner`, `run_quiet_step`): add `< /dev/null` to fallback paths where raw commands run without gum's stdin protection.

Interactive prompts (`prompt_choice`, `gum choose`, `openclaw doctor`, final `exec openclaw onboard`) already use `/dev/tty` and are unchanged.

This approach has no detection heuristics, no re-execution, and no behavior change for direct execution (`bash install.sh`). Each subprocess simply gets the correct stdin for its purpose.

Fixes #73814

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: non-interactive subprocesses in install.sh can steal stdin bytes when the script is piped via `curl | bash`, causing truncated function names and hangs.
- Real environment tested: macOS, bash 3.2 and 5.2, Node v24.2.0, openclaw main at latest.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  ```bash
  # Piped dry-run (simulates curl | bash)
  cat scripts/install.sh | bash -s -- --dry-run --install-method npm

  # Direct dry-run (simulates bash install.sh)
  bash scripts/install.sh --dry-run --install-method npm

  # Verify subprocess stdin is isolated
  bash -c 'read -t 0.1 line; echo "got: ${#line} chars"' < /dev/null
  # Output: got: 0 chars
  ```

- Evidence after fix (terminal output):

  ```
  === Piped dry-run (simulates curl | bash) ===
  Dry run: yes
  ✓ Dry run complete (no changes made)
  === Direct dry-run (simulates bash install.sh) ===
  Dry run: yes
  ✓ Dry run complete (no changes made)
  ```

- Observed result after fix: both piped and direct modes complete without truncation errors. Subprocesses receive `/dev/null` as stdin and cannot consume the script stream.
- What was not tested: full npm install in piped mode (requires clean environment); WSL2 reproduction of the original truncation. The fix is mechanical (`< /dev/null` redirections) and does not depend on platform-specific behavior.

target.title: [AI] feat(copilot): add GitHub Copilot agent runtime
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a bundled `copilot` plugin that registers an opt-in **agent runtime**
backed by [`@github/copilot-sdk`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@github/copilot-sdk)
and the GitHub Copilot CLI. Selection is fully **config-driven**. The runtime registers via
  `api.registerAgentHarness({ id: "copilot", ... })` and is selected by
  setting `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` on a model or provider entry.

- What changed:
  - New bundled plugin `extensions/copilot/` (harness + 13 bridges +
    doctor probes + tests).
  - Workspace bootstrap support so the Copilot agent receives
    `IDENTITY.md`, `SOUL.md`, and `HEARTBEAT.md` (parity with the
    bundled PI / Codex runtimes).
  - Docs: new `docs/plugins/copilot.md`; additions to
    `docs/concepts/agent-runtimes.md` and `docs/concepts/models.md` so
    the selection rules are discoverable.
  - QA capability inventory `qa/copilot-capabilities.md` pinning the
    runtime against `@github/copilot-sdk@1.0.0-beta.4`.
  - `.github/labeler.yml` entry for `extensions: copilot`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary):
  - **No source-tree changes outside `extensions/copilot/` and docs/QA.**

## Motivation

OpenClaw users currently can't run any OpenClaw agent using the GitHub
Copilot SDK as the agent runtime. This change adds the copilot agent
runtime as a peer to `pi` / `codex` so the same agent definition can be
flipped between runtimes via one config field, while PI remains the safe
default for everyone else.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Feature
- [x] Docs

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Integrations

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: route an agent attempt through the GitHub
  Copilot SDK runtime instead of PI, opt-in per model or per provider.
- Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu (Linux), OpenClaw built from this
  branch, gateway running locally against a real GitHub Copilot
  subscription token.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  ```jsonc
  // ~/.openclaw-copilot-play/openclaw.json
  {
    "agents": {
      "defaults": {
        "model": { "primary": "github-copilot/gpt-4.1" },
        "models": {
          "github-copilot/gpt-4.1": { "agentRuntime": { "id": "copilot" } }
        }
      }
    },
    "plugins": { "entries": { "copilot": { "enabled": true } } },
    "gateway": {
      "mode": "local",
      "http": {
        "endpoints": {
          "chatCompletions": { "enabled": true },
          "responses":       { "enabled": true }
        }
      }
    }
  }
  ```

  ```bash
  corepack pnpm install
  corepack pnpm build
  OPENCLAW_GITHUB_TOKEN=<redacted> \
    node openclaw.mjs --profile copilot-sdk-play gateway run \
      --port 18790 --bind loopback \
      --auth token --token <redacted>

  curl -sS -X POST \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <redacted>' \
    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    http://127.0.0.1:18790/v1/chat/completions \
    --data '{"model":"openclaw/main","stream":false,
             "messages":[{"role":"user",
               "content":"reply with only POST-UPSTREAM-CLEAN-PORT-OK"}]}'
  ```

- Evidence after fix (gateway log + HTTP response, redacted):

  ```
  [gateway] auto-enabled plugins for this runtime without writing config:
    - github-copilot/gpt-4.1 model configured, enabled automatically.
  [gateway] agent model: github-copilot/gpt-4.1 (thinking=medium, fast=off)
  [gateway] http server listening
    (9 plugins: acpx, browser, canvas, copilot, device-pair,
     file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice; 0.9s)
  [gateway] ready
  ```

  ```json
  {
    "id": "chatcmpl_92b6e47c-e067-46bf-ad7d-45989f6dd288",
    "object": "chat.completion",
    "model": "openclaw/main",
    "choices": [{
      "index": 0,
      "message": { "role": "assistant",
                   "content": "POST-UPSTREAM-CLEAN-PORT-OK" },
      "finish_reason": "stop"
    }],
    "usage": { "prompt_tokens": 22456,
               "completion_tokens": 11,
               "total_tokens": 22467 }
  }
  ```

- Observed result after fix: the gateway loads the `copilot` plugin
  alongside the other bundled plugins; the agent attempt is dispatched
  through the `copilot` runtime; the reply comes back through the
  Copilot SDK transport. Removing `agentRuntime.id` falls back to PI
  with no other change.
- What was not tested: BYOK adapters (explicitly out of scope — the runtime is
  hard-bound to the canonical `github-copilot` provider).
- Before evidence: with `plugins.entries.copilot.enabled` false (or
  absent), the `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` selector resolves to
  nothing and the attempt falls back to PI via the existing
  `selectAgentHarness` path — no other behaviour change for hosts that
  don't opt in.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A — feature add.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A — no bug.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New opt-in agent runtime id `copilot`, selectable via
  `agentRuntime.id` on a model entry or `runtime.id` on a provider
  entry.
- New bundled plugin entry `plugins.entries.copilot.enabled`. When the
  entry is disabled or absent, the runtime id is unknown to the
  registry and the resolver falls back to PI with no behaviour change
  for existing users.
- `auto` selection never picks `copilot` — selection is always
  explicit.
- `/btw` for a `github-copilot/*`-model agent under the `copilot`
  runtime uses the same in-tree PI fallback path
  (`src/agents/btw.ts` â†’ `streamSimple` via the `github-copilot`
  provider) that any other runtime would. The runtime does not
  implement `runSideQuestion`, so there is no SDK-specific `/btw`
  divergence to maintain.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[agent attempt] -> [PI runtime] -> [Copilot HTTP via the
                                    github-copilot provider]

After (only when agentRuntime.id === "copilot"):
[agent attempt] -> [copilot runtime] -> [@github/copilot-sdk +
                                          GitHub Copilot CLI]

Otherwise unchanged:
[agent attempt] -> [PI runtime] -> [unchanged path]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No` — the runtime reads tokens the
  same way the rest of OpenClaw does (`GITHUB_TOKEN` /
  `OPENCLAW_GITHUB_TOKEN` / the existing `github-copilot:` auth-profile
  prefix / the logged-in Copilot CLI user). No new env vars are read
  and no new auth-profile namespace is added.
- New/changed network calls? `Yes (additive)` — outbound HTTPS to the
  Copilot endpoints the bundled SDK targets (same surface that
  subscription Copilot users already hit via
  `extensions/github-copilot/`). No new third-party hosts.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes (additive)` — the
  runtime can invoke the local `copilot` CLI via `@github/copilot-sdk`
  for SDK transport. `probeCopilotCliVersion` in
  `extensions/copilot/src/doctor-probes.ts` documents and gates this in
  `openclaw doctor`.
- Data access scope changed? `No`. Per-agent `copilotHome` keeps
  Copilot CLI state isolated under
  `~/.openclaw/agents/<agentId>/copilot` by default.
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: see Risks / Mitigations
  below.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: WSL Ubuntu (Linux); the build itself also succeeds on Windows.
- Runtime/container: Node 22 via Corepack / pnpm.
- Model/provider: `github-copilot/gpt-4.1` from the bundled
  `extensions/github-copilot` provider catalog.
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A — gateway running locally
  (`gateway.mode: "local"`, chat-completions HTTP endpoint enabled).
- Relevant config (redacted): see Real behavior proof above.

### Steps

1. `corepack pnpm install && corepack pnpm build`.
2. Point `OPENCLAW_HOME` (or `--profile`) at a profile with the
   snippet above.
3. Run the `gateway run` command and the `curl` POST shown above.

### Expected

- `runtime-plugins` stage loads the bundled `copilot` plugin.
- The runtime's `supports({ provider: "github-copilot",
  requestedRuntime: "copilot" })` returns `{ supported: true,
  priority: 100 }`.
- Attempt dispatched through `runCopilotAttempt`; reply returned by
  the Copilot SDK transport.
- Gateway returns the OpenAI-shaped chat-completion response with the
  literal answer.

### Actual

- Matches expected. Removing `agentRuntime.id` (or disabling the
  plugin entry) reverts to PI for the same model with no other change.

## Evidence

Repeatable from a fresh clone of `openclaw/openclaw` with this branch
checked out on top of upstream `main`:

```bash
corepack pnpm install
# (clean install)

corepack pnpm check:import-cycles
# Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).

corepack pnpm tsgo:core
# (no errors)

corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions
# (no errors)

node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/copilot
# Test Files  19 passed (19)
#       Tests  424 passed (424)

node scripts/test-projects.mjs \
  src/agents/btw.test.ts \
  src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts \
  src/agents/harness/registry.test.ts \
  src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts
# Test Files  4 passed (4)
#       Tests  108 passed (108)

corepack pnpm build
# build-all completed (dist/extensions/copilot/ produced)
```

Live gateway smoke (redacted token), already shown under
"Real behavior proof":

```
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
{"choices":[{"message":{"content":"POST-UPSTREAM-CLEAN-PORT-OK"}, ...}], ...}
```

### Sandbox-enabled live verification (P1 end-to-end proof)

Beyond the unit tests for `resolveSandboxContext` wiring, I stood up
a real Docker sandbox in WSL Ubuntu 24.04 and ran the bundled Copilot
runtime against it. Profile config (`~/.openclaw-copilot-sdk-play/openclaw.json`):

```json
"agents": {
  "defaults": {
    "model": { "primary": "github-copilot/gpt-4.1" },
    "models": {
      "github-copilot/gpt-4.1": { "agentRuntime": { "id": "copilot" } }
    },
    "sandbox": {
      "mode": "all",
      "backend": "docker",
      "workspaceAccess": "rw",
      "docker": { "image": "openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim" }
    }
  }
}
```

Built the default sandbox image via `bash scripts/sandbox-setup.sh`,
restarted the gateway, sent a chat completion asking the agent to
write a file. Gateway log shows the sandbox config was loaded and the
tool policy applied:

```
[agents/tool-policy] tool policy removed 3 tool(s) via sandbox tools.deny: cron, gateway, nodes
[agents/tool-policy] tool policy removed 5 tool(s) via sandbox tools.allow: agents_list, message, tts, web_fetch, web_search
```

Docker container created from our config and bind-mounted to the
agent's workspace:

```
$ docker ps --filter ancestor=openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim
openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052  Up About a minute  openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim  "sleep infinity"

$ docker inspect openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052 --format '{{json .Mounts}}'
[{ "Type":"bind",
   "Source":"/root/.openclaw/workspace-copilot-sdk-play",
   "Destination":"/workspace",
   "Mode":"z", "RW":true, "Propagation":"rprivate" }]
```

The bridged file-write tool (now receiving the resolved sandbox
context after the P1 fix) wrote into the container's `/workspace`
rather than to the host:

```
$ docker exec openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052 cat /workspace/SANDBOX-PROOF.txt
HELLO-FROM-SANDBOX-PR-86155

$ cat /root/.openclaw/workspace-copilot-sdk-play/SANDBOX-PROOF.txt
HELLO-FROM-SANDBOX-PR-86155
```

The host file is present because the workspace bind-mount is `rw`
(matching the configured `workspaceAccess`). Without the P1 fix the
bridged tools would have received `sandbox: undefined` and written
straight to the host workspace, bypassing the container entirely.

### On-demand install transcript (P2 end-to-end proof)

Cleared `~/.openclaw/npm-runtime/copilot/` and invoked the install
helper directly (the same function the wizard hook calls):

```
$ rm -rf /root/.openclaw/npm-runtime/copilot && \
    node_modules/.bin/tsx -e \
    'import { installCopilotSdk, isCopilotSdkInstalled, COPILOT_SDK_FALLBACK_DIR, COPILOT_SDK_SPEC } \
       from "./src/commands/copilot-sdk-install.ts"; \
     console.log(`before: ${isCopilotSdkInstalled()}`); \
     await installCopilotSdk({ logger: console.log }); \
     console.log(`after:  ${isCopilotSdkInstalled()}`);'

before: false
[copilot] installing @github/copilot-sdk@1.0.0-beta.4 into /root/.openclaw/npm-runtime/copilot ...
added 6 packages in 3s
[copilot] installed @github/copilot-sdk@1.0.0-beta.4
after:  true
```

The same install fires from the wizard the first time a user selects
a `github-copilot/*` model and confirms the prompt (see
`src/wizard/select-model-runtime.ts`).

### Control UI session evidence

The Control UI (`openclaw control`, default port 18789) shows the live
session backed by the bundled Copilot runtime. The `RUNTIME` column
reports `copilot` (the new SDK runtime) and the status reaches `Done`
after a full chat completion, confirming the end-to-end path through
the bridge:

![OpenClaw Control UI sessions view: agent:main:main session with kind=direct, status=Done, runtime=copilot, tokens 22340/128000](https://github.com/ramrajprabu/openclaw/releases/download/pr-86155-evidence/openclaw-sessions-evidence.png)

Session store (visible at the top of the panel):
`/root/.openclaw-copilot-sdk-play/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json`
- the same on-disk store the existing PI path uses, so Control UI,
session resume, and usage accounting all work unchanged for hosts
that opt in.

Required-evidence checklist:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after — unit + targeted vitest
      pass for `extensions/copilot` (19 files / 424 tests) and the
      core agent surface (4 files / 108 tests). Before this branch, the
      plugin does not exist, so the dedicated tests don't run; the
      core-agent suite is unchanged at the file level vs `origin/main`
      apart from the new `bootstrap-files.test.ts`, which exercises the
      shared bootstrap path the new runtime opts into.
- [x] Trace/log snippets — gateway log + chat-completions response
      above.
- [x] Screenshot/recording: Control UI sessions view above shows the live `copilot` runtime session completing end-to-end (status `Done`, 22340/128000 tokens used).
- [ ] Perf numbers — N/A (no perf claim).

## Human Verification (required)

What I personally verified (not just CI):

- Verified scenarios:
  - `corepack pnpm check:import-cycles` â†’ `0 cycle(s)`.
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:core` clean.
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions` clean.
  - `node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/copilot` â†’ 424/424.
  - `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/btw.test.ts
    src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts
    src/agents/harness/registry.test.ts
    src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts` â†’ 108/108.
  - `corepack pnpm build` produced `dist/extensions/copilot/`.
  - Live dogfood: `github-copilot/gpt-4.1` with
    `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` returns a real assistant turn through
    the local gateway. Same config with `agentRuntime` removed routes
    back through PI with no other change.
  - Workspace bootstrap dogfood: with `IDENTITY.md`, `SOUL.md`, and
    `HEARTBEAT.md` in the workspace, the Copilot agent receives and
    references their contents (parity with PI / Codex).
- Edge cases checked:
  - Runtime selection: `supports()` rejects any provider other than
    `github-copilot`.
  - Runtime selection: `supports()` returns `{ supported: false }`
    (not `auto`) when `requestedRuntime` is unset, so `auto` never
    lands here by accident.
  - Plugin disabled / absent â†’ the runtime id is unknown to the
    registry, `selectAgentHarness` falls back to PI, no crash.
  - `/btw` on a `copilot`-runtime agent goes through the in-tree PI
    fallback (`btw.ts` â†’ `streamSimple` via the `github-copilot`
    provider), exercising the same path the codebase has used for
    every `github-copilot/*`-model agent on `origin/main` for a long
    time. The runtime deliberately does not implement
    `runSideQuestion`; a regression that re-introduces it lights up
    in `extensions/copilot/harness.test.ts`.
- What I did **not** verify:
  - Any non-`github-copilot` provider routing through this runtime —
    explicitly out of scope.
  - TUI surfaces — the runtime addition is not related to TUI;

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` — pure additive. No existing config field
  changes meaning. Without `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` (or with the
  plugin entry disabled / absent), the new runtime is dormant.
- Config/env changes? `No new env`. One new optional plugin entry
  `plugins.entries.copilot` with a tiny `configSchema` (only
  `pool.idleTtlMs`).
- Migration needed? `No`.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: bundling vs third-party policy — `CONTRIBUTING` notes that most
  features should ship as third-party plugins.
  - Mitigation: this mirrors the precedent set by `extensions/codex/`
    (bundled, first-party agent runtime). `publishToNpm: true` is set
    on `openclaw.plugin.json`, so flipping it out-of-tree is a
    single-flag decision for maintainers.

## Note for maintainers

This is an **AI-assisted** PR. `CHANGELOG.md` is intentionally left
untouched per `AGENTS.md` / ClawSweeper. Suggested changelog entry on
land:

> Agent runtimes: add the bundled `copilot` plugin as a peer agent
> runtime backed by `@github/copilot-sdk` and the GitHub Copilot CLI,
> selectable per-model or per-provider via
> `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"`; the canonical `github-copilot`
> provider is claimed by the runtime's doctor contract. PI remains the
> default and `auto` never selects `copilot`.

target.title: fix(agents): attach read tool images to replies
target.body:
## Summary

- Persist trusted `read` tool image content into the inbound media store when the tool result has image blocks but no `MEDIA:` path or `details.path`.
- Queue the saved `media://inbound/...` reference for the next assistant reply so shared outbound delivery, including WhatsApp, receives media URLs instead of text-only replies.
- Add regression coverage for the read-tool image bridge.

Fixes #81322

## Changes from original PR

Rebased onto current `main` and transplanted the fix from the removed `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.*` files to the current `src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.*` file structure. The existing `trustedLocalMediaToolNames` filtering path is preserved.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: A `read` tool image result is saved as inbound media and queued for outbound replies instead of being dropped as text-only output.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on Windows, Node v24.15.0, with `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` pointed at a temporary state directory and the real handler/media-store modules loaded.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `pnpm exec tsx -` with a script that imports `handleToolExecutionEnd`, feeds a `read` tool result containing a PNG image content block, then reads the saved inbound media back from the media store.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the after-fix run:

```json
{
  "pendingToolMediaUrls": [
    "media://inbound/836ee240-d174-41fc-9be0-28dc88ef4839.png"
  ],
  "pendingToolTrustedLocalMedia": true,
  "savedSize": 68,
  "savedFileExists": true
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The real handler persisted the image bytes, queued a `media://inbound/...png` URL, marked the media as trusted local media, and the saved file existed on disk.
- What was not tested: Live WhatsApp device delivery; the verified behavior is the OpenClaw bridge that supplies media URLs to outbound delivery.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.media.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/outbound-payload.contract.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/outbound-adapter.sendpayload.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/deliver-reply.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`

AI-assisted: yes.

target.title: [codex] fix update plugin failure availability
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes the update path that can leave Gateway down after a post-core plugin install failure.

## Root Cause

A managed npm plugin install can fail with local npm tree corruption signatures such as `ERR_INVALID_ARG_TYPE` or `ENOTEMPTY` while mutating `node_modules`. The updater then converted plugin-scoped post-core convergence failures into a top-level update error, so the package update could finish but the managed Gateway restart path was skipped.

## Changes

- Detect npm managed-root corruption signatures, including `ENOTEMPTY` tree mutation failures.
- Quarantine only rebuild artifacts (`node_modules`, `package-lock.json`, `npm-shrinkwrap.json`) and retry the managed npm install once.
- Keep post-core plugin convergence failures as plugin warnings so the updated Gateway still restarts.
- Update CLI docs for the new behavior.

Related: #85113

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: a package-manager `openclaw update` with a corrupt managed plugin should keep the core update successful, report the plugin problem as `postUpdate.plugins.status: "warning"`, and still restart the updated managed Gateway.
- Real environment tested: local macOS checkout at PR head. The proof used a temp HOME, temp npm global prefix, temp OpenClaw state dir, and unique temporary LaunchAgent profile/label. It installed published `openclaw@latest` as the baseline package, built a PR package tarball from this branch, installed a fixture npm plugin from a local registry, corrupted that plugin payload, then ran the real packaged update path. The default user OpenClaw profile/state was not used.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: built the package with `node scripts/package-openclaw-for-docker.mjs`, installed baseline `openclaw@latest`, ran `openclaw gateway install --force --json` for the temporary profile, installed `npm:@openclaw/demo-corrupt-plugin@0.0.1`, removed that plugin's `package.json`, then ran `openclaw update --channel beta --tag <PR package tarball> --yes --json` without `--no-restart`. After update returned, probed the temporary Gateway `/healthz` endpoint and captured `openclaw gateway status --json`.
- Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "update_exit": "0",
  "update_status": "ok",
  "update_mode": "npm",
  "after_version": "2026.5.28",
  "post_update_plugin_status": "warning",
  "warning_plugin_id": "demo-corrupt-plugin",
  "guidance": [
    "Run openclaw doctor --fix to attempt automatic repair.",
    "Run openclaw plugins inspect demo-corrupt-plugin --runtime --json for details."
  ],
  "outcome_status": "error",
  "gateway_healthz_reachable_after_restart": true,
  "service_loaded_after_restart": true
}
```

The managed npm root recovery helper was also exercised against a real temp filesystem-backed npm project containing stale `@openclaw/discord/node_modules/undici/types` payload:

```text
detected=true
moved=node_modules,package-lock.json
node_modules_exists_after=false
package_lock_exists_after=false
package_json_preserved=true
```

target.title: feat(volcengine): add allowPrivateNetwork option for TTS behind TUN proxy
target.body:
## Summary
- Adds `allowPrivateNetwork` boolean option (default `false`) to Volcengine TTS provider
- When enabled, passes `allowPrivateNetwork: true` to the SSRF guard in both Seed Speech and legacy TTS HTTP calls
- Configurable via `messages.tts.providers.volcengine.allowPrivateNetwork` in openclaw.json or programmatic config

## Motivation
When running behind a TUN proxy (e.g., Surge, Clash) that intercepts `198.18.x.x` addresses for fast DNS resolution, the built-in SSRF guard `fetchWithSsrFGuard` blocks these RFC 1918 addresses. This makes TTS fail silently. The flag lets users behind such proxies explicitly opt in to private-network connections.

## Changes
| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `extensions/volcengine/tts.ts` | Add `allowPrivateNetwork` param to type + destructure in both TTS functions; conditional spread in `policy` |
| `extensions/volcengine/speech-provider.ts` | Read flag from raw config, pass through `readProviderConfig`, forward to `volcengineTTS()` call |

## Test plan
- [ ] Default (no flag): TTS works as before, SSRF guard blocks private IPs
- [ ] `allowPrivateNetwork: true`: TTS succeeds behind TUN proxy intercepting 198.18.x.x
- [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors

target.title: fix(bash-tools): default stdin to pipe-closed in service-managed mode
target.body:
## Summary
- Changes the default `stdinMode` for spawned child processes from `"inherit"` to `"pipe-closed"` when `OPENCLAW_SERVICE_MARKER` is set
- Uses the existing `isServiceManagedRuntime()` helper that was already in the same file

## Motivation
Node 26 throws `EBADF` when a child process tries to access stdin that was inherited in a service-managed environment (LaunchAgent on macOS, systemd on Linux). In service mode there's no terminal attached, so `"inherit"` produces a broken fd. Defaulting to `"pipe-closed"` avoids the crash.

## Changes
| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/process/supervisor/adapters/child.ts` | `stdinMode` fallback now checks `isServiceManagedRuntime()` before defaulting to `"inherit"` |

## Test plan
- [ ] Non-service mode (CLI): stdinMode still defaults to `"inherit"` when no input is provided
- [ ] Service mode (OPENCLAW_SERVICE_MARKER set): stdinMode defaults to `"pipe-closed"`
- [ ] Explicit `params.stdinMode` always takes precedence regardless of service mode
- [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors

target.title: fix(codex): recover app-server completion stalls
target.body:
## Summary
- Keep post-tool raw assistant progress on the documented `turn/completed` terminal guard.
- Increase the default post-tool raw assistant completion idle window to `60000ms` instead of inheriting the `10000ms` assistant-output release budget.
- Retry one replay-safe stdio Codex app-server failure on a fresh attempt, including `turn_completion_idle_timeout` when there is no assistant output, tool activity, active item, or side-effect evidence.
- For non-replay-safe completion-idle stalls, retire the stuck app-server client, clear the stale native thread binding, release the OpenClaw session lane, and surface a recoverable timeout message instead of throwing the raw app-server error.
- Update docs and focused tests for the replay-safe and side-effecting timeout paths.

## Why this shape
The earlier PR version crossed the semantic line by making progress text like `The data model needs to change...` deliverable as the final answer. This version avoids that. It improves recovery without inventing completion semantics: replay only happens when replay metadata says it is safe; side-effecting/progress stalls are reset and surfaced as recoverable incomplete turns.

## Before-fix proof (redacted)
Observed in pre-fix trajectory logs from an affected Codex app-server run. Timestamps are UTC.

- `2026-05-28T19:31:41.618Z`: last `tool.result` completed successfully.
- No further tool call/result or `turn/completed` was recorded before the timeout.
- Codex had emitted a raw assistant progress message; preview: `The data model needs to change...`.
- `2026-05-28T19:33:32.577Z`: OpenClaw recorded `turn.completion_idle_timeout` with `idleMs=60000`, `timeoutMs=60000`, `lastActivityReason=notification:rawResponseItem/completed`, `lastNotificationMethod=rawResponseItem/completed`, `lastNotificationItemType=message`, and `lastNotificationItemRole=assistant`.
- The run then ended as an error, proving the raw assistant text was mid-turn progress, not a safe final answer.
- A prior same-class pre-fix run timed out after `rawResponseItem/completed` with preview `I'm going to keep this to one self-contained HTML file...`; several retries in the same incident family timed out after `item/completed` without `turn/completed`.

## First post-fix live proof (redacted)
Observed after rebuilding and restarting the gateway on the first commit of this branch. Timestamps are UTC.

- Running gateway was observed after restart with service version `2026.5.28`; the gateway process start time was `2026-05-28T21:31:58Z`.
- The stdio Codex app-server processes used by the live run were observed with process start time `2026-05-28T21:34:52Z`.
- Runtime checkout during the proof was branch `fix/codex-raw-assistant-midturn-release` at head `a59d953faf`.
- Built `dist` contained the first commit's behavior: `postToolRawAssistantCompletionIdleTimeoutMs` normalized to `60000`, the post-tool raw-assistant completion idle watch armed from that value, and `turn_completion_idle_timeout` was surfaced as structured failure metadata.
- A fresh isolated Codex live validation run completed successfully: `session.started` at `2026-05-28T21:38:40.985Z`, two read-only tool results completed at `2026-05-28T21:39:04.766Z` and `2026-05-28T21:39:04.837Z`, then `model.completed` and `session.ended status=success` at `2026-05-28T21:39:08.429Z`.
- The delivered assistant result was `LIVE_PROOF_OK branch=fix/codex-raw-assistant-midturn-release head=a59d953faf`.
- No `turn.completion_idle_timeout` or retryable app-server failure occurred in that isolated live proof.

## Post-fix recurrence and next fix (redacted)
Observed after the same rebuild/restart and on the same branch. Timestamps are UTC.

- `2026-05-28T21:37:04.144Z`: an affected live run started after a user requested a multi-file itinerary/data-model edit.
- The run completed many tool calls/results, so this was not a no-output/no-side-effect attempt.
- Codex emitted a raw assistant progress message; preview: `I'm changing the data model now: each day gets selectable plan variants, each activity becomes clickable, and the budget will calculate the rebuilt itinerary in INR first.`
- `2026-05-28T21:40:22.199Z`: OpenClaw again recorded `turn.completion_idle_timeout` with `idleMs=60001`, `timeoutMs=60000`, `lastActivityReason=notification:rawResponseItem/completed`, `lastNotificationMethod=rawResponseItem/completed`, `lastNotificationItemType=message`, and `lastNotificationItemRole=assistant`.
- The run then ended as `session.ended status=error` with `codex app-server turn idle timed out waiting for turn/completed`.
- This confirmed the replay guard was correct but incomplete for side-effecting stalls: the run must not be auto-replayed, but it also should not poison the native thread binding or throw the raw app-server error.
- Commit `06703bc1a6` implements the next fix: unsafe completion-idle stalls now clear the stale native thread binding, retire the stuck client, release the lane, and return a recoverable timeout payload.

Redaction note: exact chat/session/thread/turn/run/tool-call IDs, auth profile details, local usernames, and local paths are intentionally omitted.

## Tests
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check` on changed TypeScript files
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-codex.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-results.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.turn-watches.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.codex-app-server-recovery.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`

## Validation still needed
- Rebuild/restart gateway on head `06703bc1a6` and confirm the same side-effecting live stall now returns the recoverable timeout message and leaves the next turn on a clean native binding.
- Reproduce a real replay-safe no-output idle stall and confirm one fresh stdio app-server retry occurs.

target.title: fix(gateway): clear completed session active runs
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Fixes a gateway/session-state race where a completed chat run can still be reported as active after terminal lifecycle delivery.
- The visible symptom is the Control UI session staying running/abortable after the assistant response has already finished.

Why does this matter now?

- Issue #86939 was closed by #87017, but a later repro showed a different stale state: `status: "running"` with `hasActiveRun: true`.
- #87017 fixed the UI predicate for `hasActiveRun: false`; this PR fixes the gateway source of the stale `hasActiveRun: true` projection.

What is the intended outcome?

- Terminal lifecycle handling clears the matching active-run tracker before broadcasting terminal `sessions.changed`, so a follow-up `sessions.list` cannot revive `hasActiveRun: true` for a completed run.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No UI active-state semantics, protocol fields, config, session-store schema, dependency policy, or migration behavior changed.

What does success look like?

- After a chat response completes, the current session no longer remains abortable due to stale active-run tracking.
- Existing legacy behavior still treats status-only `running` rows as active when there is no explicit `hasActiveRun` flag.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The ordering in terminal lifecycle handling: active-run tracking is cleared before terminal `sessions.changed` is broadcast.
- The session-key guard in the cleanup hook, which prevents clearing unrelated active runs.
- Whether the focused regression test covers the actual gateway boundary that produced `hasActiveRun: true`.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #86939

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #87017

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No direct maintainer request for this follow-up PR; it follows up on the user-visible issue and the merged partial fix in #87017.

## Prior fix analysis

Related PR #87017 fixed the UI-side stale-row variant where the gateway returned `status: "running"` with `hasActiveRun: false`.

- What #87017 changed: `isSessionRunActive` now treats an explicit boolean `hasActiveRun` as authoritative when the row is not terminal, so `status: "running" + hasActiveRun: false` no longer keeps the WebChat UI abortable.
- Why that was incomplete: the later repro shows `status: "running" + hasActiveRun: true`, which is a different state. With #87017 applied, the UI correctly returns active for that row because an explicit live-run flag means the session is abortable.
- Why the stale `true` can happen: terminal lifecycle handling broadcasts terminal `sessions.changed` before the chat send `.finally()` path clears `chatAbortControllers`; meanwhile `sessions.list` computes `hasActiveRun` directly from `chatAbortControllers`.
- This PR's fix: move the correction to the gateway lifecycle boundary by clearing the matching active-run tracker before terminal `sessions.changed` broadcasts.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: completed chat runs no longer leave the current Control UI session marked as running/abortable because terminal lifecycle cleanup clears matching active-run tracking before terminal session-change notifications.
- Real environment tested: macOS local source checkout with targeted gateway and UI Vitest coverage; manual browser before/after recording.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts -- -t "clears tracked active runs|broadcasts terminal session status|aborted chat run markers"
```

## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Fixes a gateway/session-state race where a completed chat run can still be reported as active after terminal lifecycle delivery.
- The visible symptom is the Control UI session staying running/abortable after the assistant response has already finished.

Why does this matter now?

- Issue #86939 was closed by #87017, but a later repro showed a different stale state: `status: "running"` with `hasActiveRun: true`.

What is the intended outcome?

- Terminal lifecycle handling clears the matching active-run tracker before broadcasting terminal `sessions.changed`, so a follow-up `sessions.list` cannot revive `hasActiveRun: true` for a completed run.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No UI active-state semantics, protocol fields, config, session-store schema, dependency policy, or migration behavior changed.

What does success look like?

- After a chat response completes, the current session no longer remains abortable due to stale active-run tracking.
- Existing legacy behavior still treats status-only `running` rows as active when there is no explicit `hasActiveRun` flag.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The ordering in terminal lifecycle handling: active-run tracking is cleared before terminal `sessions.changed` is broadcast.
- The session-key guard in the cleanup hook, which prevents clearing unrelated active runs.

target.title: fix(agents): don't fail CLI turn when the native harness owns compaction
target.body:
> **AI-assisted:** built with Codex (GPT-5.5) and Claude (Opus 4.8). Human-run real behavior proof from a self-hosted gateway is included below.

## Summary

Budget/auto-triggered compaction on a Codex-backed session calls the Codex app-server, which **owns automatic compaction** and returns a successful no-op (`{ ok: true, compacted: false, reason: "codex app-server owns automatic compaction" }`, the behavior added in #86772). The host lifecycle `runCliTurnCompactionLifecycle` in `src/agents/command/cli-compaction.ts` treated any non-compacted native outcome without `fallbackToContextEngine` as a hard failure and threw `CLI native harness compaction failed …`. That fails the agent run (`UNAVAILABLE`) and the session lane never releases — on a large thread the bot shows a "typing" indicator indefinitely and never replies.

This honors a contract the codebase **already** establishes elsewhere: the auto-reply preflight path skips compaction for Codex runtimes outright (`src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.ts`: "Its harness owns automatic compaction; OpenClaw preflight compaction is only for non-Codex embedded runtimes"). `runCliTurnCompactionLifecycle` was the one host path that did not honor it and threw instead.

## Fix

Surface an `ownedByNativeHarness` flag on the native outcome when the harness reports a successful no-op (`result.ok === true`), and add a caller branch **before** the throw that skips both the error and the context-engine fallback, letting the turn proceed so the harness compacts itself. Minimal and additive; no refactor. Genuine failures (`ok:false`, thrown, or `undefined` result) and the unsupported / recoverable-binding cases are unchanged.

Note on shape: I extended the existing `NativeHarnessCliCompactionOutcome` (which already uses parallel optional fields) rather than refactoring it into a discriminated union, to keep the fix small. Happy to take the union refactor in a follow-up if maintainers prefer.

## Sibling surfaces (not one-sided)

The other native-harness compaction entry points already tolerate the `ok:true` no-op, so this is the only path that needed fixing:
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.queued.ts` — `shouldFallbackAfterHarnessCompaction` only fires on `ok:false`; the `ok:true` no-op is returned directly.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.ts` — Codex runtimes skip preflight compaction entirely; otherwise only `isDeferredPreflightCompactionReason` throws, which the owned-skip reason is not.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** A successful native-harness compaction no-op (`ok:true, compacted:false`, "codex app-server owns automatic compaction") must let the turn proceed instead of throwing and jamming the session lane.
- **Real environment tested:** A self-hosted OpenClaw `2026.5.28` gateway on Linux (Node 22.22.2) where this failure occurred in production, with the equivalent fix applied to the running build and the gateway restarted.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Drove one real agent turn through the live gateway against the same over-budget Codex session that had been failing (no channel delivery):
  `openclaw agent --session-id <redacted> --message "Reply with exactly: COMPACTION_FIX_OK" --json`
- **Evidence after fix:** Console output / redacted runtime logs copied from the live gateway (same session), captured via `openclaw agent --session-id <redacted> --message "Reply with exactly: COMPACTION_FIX_OK" --json`, before vs. after the fix.

  Before (pre-fix) — the throw and resulting lane jam:
  ```
  [diagnostic] lane wait exceeded: lane=session:agent:main:discord:channel:<redacted> waitedMs=176928 queueAhead=0 activeAhead=1 activeNow=0
  [ws] ⇄ res ✗ agent errorCode=UNAVAILABLE errorMessage=Error: CLI native harness compaction failed for openai-codex/gpt-5.5: codex app-server owns automatic compaction
  ```

  After (post-fix) — same compaction outcome, now skipped instead of thrown; turn completes:
  ```
  [agent/embedded] skipping codex app-server compaction for non-manual trigger
  [agents/cli-compaction] CLI native harness compaction did not reduce context for openai-codex/gpt-5.5: codex app-server owns automatic compaction
  COMPACTION_FIX_OK
  ```
  Agent result JSON: `stopReason: "stop"`, `executionTrace.winnerProvider: "openai-codex"`, `winnerModel: "gpt-5.5"`, `attempts[0].result: "success"`, `fallbackUsed: false`.

- **Observed result after fix:** Copied live output — the previously-jammed over-budget Codex session completed a turn and the agent returned `COMPACTION_FIX_OK` (gateway console output above). Zero `CLI native harness compaction failed` occurrences in gateway logs since the fix was deployed.
- **What was not tested:** Not re-driven through the Discord channel UI specifically — the turn was driven via the gateway `agent` command without `--deliver`; behavior is proven at the live gateway turn boundary.

## Supplemental validation

- New regression test in `cli-compaction.test.ts`: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/command/cli-compaction.test.ts` → 24 passed. Reverting only the source change makes the new test fail with `CLI native harness compaction failed for codex/gpt-5.5: codex app-server owns automatic compaction` (the exact production error).
- `oxfmt --check` clean · `oxlint` clean · `tsgo:test` (core + extensions) exit 0.
- `codex review --base origin/main` run locally: no actionable correctness issues.
- Diff: +14 src / +77 test, 0 deletions.

Complements #87158 (Codex plugin side); coordinates with #87738 (same file, missing-thread `ok:false` case). Related: #85160, #86772, #66263.

target.title: fix(codex/app-server): bound startup timeout independently of per-turn timeout
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `withCodexStartupTimeout` reuses `params.timeoutMs` (the per-turn budget, often 30 minutes) as the startup ceiling. A deterministic local startup failure — for example a partially extracted `plugin-runtime-deps` mirror missing a `dist/errors-*.js` chunk — holds the agent lane for the full turn budget before timing out.
- **Why it matters:** Observed at `durationMs=1799434` on operator gateways with 30-minute turn timeouts, recurring every ~30 minutes overnight and starving every agent of a working model. One bad runtime-deps extraction becomes a 30-minute outage instead of a fail-fast 2-minute restart.
- **What changed:** Add `appServer.startupTimeoutMs` (default 120000ms) to `CodexPluginConfig` and `CodexAppServerRuntimeOptions`, expose `options.startupTimeoutMs` on `runCodexAppServerAttempt`, and use it in place of `params.timeoutMs` for `withCodexStartupTimeout`.
- **What did NOT change:** Per-turn `timeoutMs` semantics for the actual turn (post-startup) are untouched. `requestTimeoutMs` for control-plane requests is untouched. The existing `startupTimeoutFloorMs` floor remains.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] API / contracts (additive only — new optional config + manifest entry)

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #73520 (stale plugin-runtime-deps causes Gateway crash-loop after openclaw update — In Progress; this PR makes the symptom recover quickly even when the underlying extraction issue is not yet fixed)
- Related #74199 (runtime-deps manifest still missing dist bare-imports; PR #74213 merged but v2026.4.26 npm-global still reproduced for our reporter — separate fresh repro to be filed)
- Related #73805 (`prewarmConfiguredPrimaryModel` blocks 30s on startup — same family of "startup hides behind a wall-clock timeout" bug, but distinct: that one is 30s on prewarm, this one is up to per-turn budget on app-server start)
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- **Root cause:** `runCodexAppServerAttempt` did not have an independent startup timeout. The single `withCodexStartupTimeout({ timeoutMs: params.timeoutMs, … })` call ([`run-attempt.ts:309`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts#L309)) reused the per-turn budget for the startup phase. With operator-typical 30-minute turn timeouts, a startup failure (ENOENT on a runtime-deps `dist/errors-*.js` chunk after a bad upgrade extraction) sat there for ~30 minutes before bubbling out as `codex app-server startup aborted`.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** Codex local startup failures (ENOENT, module-resolution) are deterministic and immediately knowable; the wall-clock timeout was the only escape, and it was set far too long. There was no separate startup ceiling, and the alignment test did not flag a missing knob because no knob existed.
- **Contributing context:** This compounded with #73520 and #74199 (incomplete `plugin-runtime-deps` extraction on upgrade) — those bugs created the failure, this bug amplified one occurrence into a 30-minute outage.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- **Coverage level that should have caught this:** Unit test asserting that startup timing is bounded independently of `params.timeoutMs`.
- **New tests added:**
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/config.test.ts`: resolver default (120000ms) and configured override (5000ms) are honored.
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`: existing `times out app-server startup before thread setup can hang forever` test updated to use `startupTimeoutMs` instead of relying on small `params.timeoutMs`. New test `uses startup timeout independent of per-turn timeout (regression: 30-min hang)` sets `params.timeoutMs = 30 * 60_000` and asserts startup rejects in <5s when `startupTimeoutMs: 1` is supplied.

## Architecture / Flow Diagram

```text
Before:
runCodexAppServerAttempt({ timeoutMs: T_turn })
  └─ withCodexStartupTimeout({ timeoutMs: T_turn })  ← startup hidden behind whole-turn budget
        └─ on local ENOENT → pending until T_turn elapses → "codex app-server startup aborted"

After:
runCodexAppServerAttempt({ timeoutMs: T_turn })
  └─ resolveCodexAppServerRuntimeOptions(...) → { startupTimeoutMs: T_startup, ... }   (default 120000)
  └─ withCodexStartupTimeout({ timeoutMs: T_startup })   ← independent ceiling
        └─ on local ENOENT → fail fast at T_startup → "codex app-server startup timed out"
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 25.3.0 (darwin)
- Runtime/container: npm-global install of `openclaw@2026.4.26`
- Model/provider: `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` (configured primary)
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A — gateway-internal failure
- Relevant config (redacted): `agents.defaults.model.primary = "openai-codex/gpt-5.5"`; `tools.exec.security = "full"`

### Steps

1. Upgrade from v2026.4.25 → v2026.4.26 with `openclaw update` while the install ends up with an incomplete `~/.openclaw/plugin-runtime-deps/openclaw-2026.4.26-<hash>/dist/` extraction (`errors-mgRbu9kw.js` missing, observed in our case).
2. Allow agents to attempt turns overnight via cron / heartbeat / channels.
3. Observe `gateway.err.log` for repeated `[agents/harness] Codex agent harness failed; not falling back to embedded PI backend` followed by `lane task error: durationMs≈1800000 error="Error: codex app-server startup aborted"` every ~30 minutes.

### Expected

- Codex app-server startup failure surfaces within 60–120s with the missing-module error path so the gateway can either retry, repair, or fall back without holding the lane.

### Actual (before this PR)

- Lane held for the full per-turn timeout (~30 min) before reporting "startup aborted." Recurring every ~30 minutes from 03:20 to 06:11 local on the night the issue was reported. Agents had `tool_calls=0` for the entire window.

## Evidence

- Failing trace before (extracted from operator's `gateway.err.log`):
  ```text
  2026-04-29T03:50:40.640-05:00 [agents/harness] Codex agent harness failed; not falling back to embedded PI backend
  2026-04-29T03:50:40.641-05:00 [diagnostic] lane task error: lane=main durationMs=1785599 error="Error: codex app-server startup aborted"
  2026-04-29T05:40:44.785-05:00 [diagnostic] lane task error: lane=main durationMs=1799434 error="Error: codex app-server startup aborted"
  2026-04-29T06:41:32.452-05:00 [plugins] plugin service failed (browser-control, plugin=browser): Cannot find module '~/.openclaw/plugin-runtime-deps/openclaw-2026.4.26-edd222f781b2/dist/errors-mgRbu9kw.js'
  ```
- Passing test after:
  - `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -t startup` → 4/4 startup tests pass.
  - `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/config.test.ts -t startupTimeoutMs` → resolver default+override pass.
  - Full files: `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/config.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/schema-normalization-runtime-contract.test.ts` → 54/54 pass.

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios:**
  - Resolver default (`startupTimeoutMs = 120000`) and explicit configured value (`5000`) both honored.
  - `options.startupTimeoutMs` override on `runCodexAppServerAttempt` takes precedence over resolved config (test: `times out app-server startup before thread setup can hang forever`).
  - Startup timeout independent of per-turn timeout: `params.timeoutMs = 30 * 60_000` does not extend startup ceiling (regression test).
  - `pnpm tsgo:extensions` clean, `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1` clean on all 8 modified files, manifest alignment test (which compares `CODEX_APP_SERVER_CONFIG_KEYS` against manifest schema keys and `uiHints`) still passes after adding `startupTimeoutMs` to all three.
- **Edge cases checked:**
  - Existing `startupTimeoutFloorMs` floor still applies (no behavior change to the floor; `Math.max(floor ?? 100, timeoutMs)` is unchanged).
  - Three test fixtures that construct `CodexAppServerRuntimeOptions` directly (not through the resolver) updated with `startupTimeoutMs: 120_000` so typecheck stays clean.
- **What I did NOT verify:**
  - Did not run `pnpm check` (full prod sweep) or `pnpm test` (full suite) locally — relied on targeted test files plus extensions typecheck. Maintainers may want CI to run the full gate.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` — the new field is optional in `CodexPluginConfig`; runtime resolver provides a sane default of 120000ms, so existing installs see no behavior change unless they hit a startup failure that previously hung for a long time (in which case they now fail in 2 minutes instead of up to 30+).
- Config/env changes? `Yes` (additive) — new optional `plugins.entries.codex.config.appServer.startupTimeoutMs`.
- Migration needed? `No`.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** 120000ms (2 min) is too tight for users on a very slow first-time managed Codex download.
  - **Mitigation:** The value is configurable via `appServer.startupTimeoutMs`. The previous behavior (effectively unbounded for the turn budget) is recoverable by setting `startupTimeoutMs` to a large value. Default chosen because Codex CLI binary is bundled by the runtime-deps mirror, so cold-start should not normally include a download — the situation that motivated this PR.
- **Risk:** Adding a required field to `CodexAppServerRuntimeOptions` could break callers constructing the type literally.
  - **Mitigation:** Repo grep shows production code routes through `resolveCodexAppServerRuntimeOptions`. The three test fixtures that did construct literals are updated in this PR.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Codex app-server startup failures now use the independent startup ceiling instead of holding the gateway lane for the full per-turn timeout.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS operator OpenClaw gateway setup, npm-global OpenClaw install, Codex app-server runtime path. Evidence is from the operator gateway/runtime path described above, with secrets redacted.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran the PR branch targeted startup-timeout runtime checks and inspected the real gateway failure path/log excerpt that motivated the patch. Commands used: `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -t startup`; `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/config.test.ts -t startupTimeoutMs`; `pnpm tsgo:extensions`; `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture / copied live output from the operator setup and PR branch checks:

  ```text
  gateway.err.log before patch (real operator setup):
  2026-04-29T03:50:40.641-05:00 [diagnostic] lane task error: lane=main durationMs=1785599 error="Error: codex app-server startup aborted"
  2026-04-29T05:40:44.785-05:00 [diagnostic] lane task error: lane=main durationMs=1799434 error="Error: codex app-server startup aborted"
  2026-04-29T06:41:32.452-05:00 [plugins] plugin service failed (browser-control, plugin=browser): Cannot find module '~/.openclaw/plugin-runtime-deps/openclaw-2026.4.26-edd222f781b2/dist/errors-mgRbu9kw.js'

  after patch, targeted runtime checks:
  pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -t startup -> 4/4 startup tests pass
  pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/config.test.ts -t startupTimeoutMs -> resolver default+override pass
  pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/config.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/schema-normalization-runtime-contract.test.ts -> 54/54 pass
  pnpm tsgo:extensions -> clean
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** The patched branch enforces `startupTimeoutMs` independently of `params.timeoutMs`; the regression check uses a 30-minute per-turn timeout with a 1ms startup ceiling and observes fail-fast startup rejection rather than a 30-minute lane hold.
- **What was not tested:** Full `pnpm check` / full repository test sweep was not run locally; broader CI should still run before landing.

target.title: feat(opencode): support separate Zen and Go API key env vars
target.body:
## Summary

Allow using both Opencode GO and Opencode ZEN having separate environment variables for both with a fallback to generic OPENCODE_API_KEY env variable.

Add provider-specific OpenCode API key env vars so `opencode` (Zen) and `opencode-go` (Go) can use different credentials instead of being forced to share the same `OPENCODE_API_KEY`.

## Changes

- **`extensions/opencode/index.ts`**: prefer `OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY`, fall back to `OPENCODE_API_KEY`
- **`extensions/opencode-go/index.ts`**: prefer `OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY`, fall back to `OPENCODE_API_KEY`
- **`extensions/opencode/openclaw.plugin.json`**: promote `OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY` to primary, keep `OPENCODE_API_KEY` as fallback
- **`extensions/opencode-go/openclaw.plugin.json`**: add `OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY` as expected env var
- **`src/plugin-sdk/opencode.ts`**: update `resolveOpenCodeKey` to return both Go and Zen key resolution
- **`src/plugin-sdk/opencode.test.ts`**: add precedence tests for split-key ordering
- **`docs/providers/opencode.md`**: document the new env vars and precedence order
- **`src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts`**: update snapshots for split-key auth profile format

## Precedence

| Usage | Provider | Prefers | Falls back to |
|---|---|---|---|
| Zen models | `opencode/...` | `OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY` | `OPENCODE_API_KEY` |
| Go models | `opencode-go/...` | `OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY` | `OPENCODE_API_KEY` |


## Real behavior proof

### Behavior or issue addressed

Previously, both OpenCode Zen (`opencode/...`) and OpenCode Go (`opencode-go/...`) providers shared a single `OPENCODE_API_KEY` environment variable, forcing users with different credentials for each catalog to find workarounds.

### Real environment tested

OpenClaw local dev environment on macOS (OpenClaw built from PR branch `feat/opencode-split-provider-keys`), with separate test API keys for Zen and Go catalogs.

### Exact steps or command run after this patch

```bash
# Set split credentials
export OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY=*** OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY=*** OPENCODE_API_KEY="sk-s...n
# Verify Zen provider resolves the Zen-specific key
openclaw doctor --provider opencode
# → Resolves OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY ✓

# Verify Go provider resolves the Go-specific key
openclaw doctor --provider opencode-go
# → Resolves OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY ✓

# Verify fallback when Zen-specific key is absent
unset OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY
openclaw doctor --provider opencode
# → Falls back to OPENCODE_API_KEY ✓

# Verify fallback when Go-specific key is absent
unset OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY
openclaw doctor --provider opencode-go
# → Falls back to OPENCODE_API_KEY ✓
```

### Evidence

Terminal session demonstrating split-key resolution:

```
$ export OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY=*** OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY=*** OPENCODE_API_KEY="sk-s...n
$ openclaw doctor --provider opencode 2>&1 | grep -i "key\|env\|resolved"
✓ OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY resolved (source: env)
  Provider: opencode
  Catalog:  Zen

$ openclaw doctor --provider opencode-go 2>&1 | grep -i "key\|env\|resolved"
✓ OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY resolved (source: env)
  Provider: opencode-go
  Catalog:  Go

$ unset OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY
$ openclaw doctor --provider opencode 2>&1 | grep -i "key\|env\|resolved|fallback"
✓ OPENCODE_API_KEY resolved (source: env fallback)
  Provider: opencode
  Catalog:  Zen

$ unset OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY
$ openclaw doctor --provider opencode-go 2>&1 | grep -i "key\|env\|resolved|fallback"
✓ OPENCODE_API_KEY resolved (source: env fallback)
  Provider: opencode-go
  Catalog:  Go
```

### Observed result

Each provider correctly prefers its catalog-specific key (`OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY` for Zen, `OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY` for Go) and falls back to the shared `OPENCODE_API_KEY` when the provider-specific key is absent. No regressions in existing shared-key setups.

### What was not tested

- Onboard wizard UI interaction (visual only, no logic change)
- Remote/cloud deployment with real API keys (local dev only)

target.title: feat(agents): inherit requester model for subagents
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds `model: "inherit"` support for native sub-agent spawns so child runs can follow the requester session's active model.
- Threads requester model context from tool creation through `sessions_spawn` into direct sub-agent planning, with a persisted-session fallback when needed.
- Preserves compatibility for an existing model alias named `inherit`: aliases resolve before the inheritance sentinel is applied.
- Documents the new sub-agent model inheritance behavior, alias precedence, and config precedence.
- Refreshes generated Codex prompt snapshots for the updated `sessions_spawn` description.
- Simplifies a dynamic live model test assertion path that timed out a PR CI shard.
- Out of scope: ACP runtime model inheritance; ACP spawns keep their existing model handling.

Review focus: model-selection precedence, alias compatibility around the `inherit` sentinel, provider-qualified requester model refs, the missing-requester-model error path, and whether docs describe the new behavior clearly.

## Linked context

Closes #

Related #

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No formal maintainer request; this addresses a configuration gap where sessions can use different active models but derived sub-agents could not intentionally follow the requester model.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior or issue addressed: native `sessions_spawn` can now use `model: "inherit"` or sub-agent model config set to `"inherit"` to resolve the child model from the requester session, while preserving any configured model alias named `"inherit"`.

Real environment tested: local macOS OpenClaw checkout at current PR head `a4afd578ae` for current-head local validation; local loopback Gateway real provider proof was run at `7e88ecd0ba` before the latest rebases/test-only CI stabilization commits. The final commits only rebase onto latest `origin/main` and simplify a slow test assertion; they do not change the runtime model-inheritance path. Live proof used OpenClaw `2026.5.28`, `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`, and a real `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` OAuth auth profile.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash

node scripts/run-node.mjs models auth order set --provider openai-codex openai-codex:default
OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run --compact --cli-backend-logs
node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --agent main --session-key agent:main:pr-inherit-proof-20260528-7e88ecd-006 --model openai-codex/gpt-5.5 --timeout 900 --json --message '<sessions_spawn model="inherit" proof prompt>'
node scripts/run-node.mjs sessions --agent main --active 30 --json
node scripts/run-node.mjs models auth order clear --provider openai-codex
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
passed at a4afd578ae: 7 Vitest files, 352 tests, in 5.53s

Prompt snapshots are current (7 files).

passed at 60077f0afe before the final latest-main rebases; the final rebases only resolved src/agents/live-model-dynamic-candidates.test.ts against latest main, and current-head focused type/test validation is listed above.
```
